title: Prior Concentration: Learning Speed Knob

runtime.expected_outcome: Small initial concentrations (~1) give fast adaptation; large concentrations (~100) lock the prior in place. Effective learning rate is visible in A-column variance over episodes.

audiences.kid: A brand-new agent learns quickly from each clue. One with strong beliefs already barely moves.
audiences.real: The prior concentration a_0 is the agent's 'pseudo-count budget'. a_0=1 is near-uniform; a_0=100 nails the prior down.
audiences.equation: Posterior variance shrinks as 1/a_0. Fewer counts -> faster learning. Tune by initialisation.
audiences.derivation: Dirichlet variance decomposes as a_i(a_0 - a_i)/(a_0^2 (a_0 + 1)). Increasing a_0 while keeping proportions fixed shrinks variance. Sets the learning-rate analog in active inference.

orchestrate.objective: Quantify how the Dirichlet concentration controls the effective learning rate.
orchestrate.context: tiny_open_goal, learning_enabled=true; vary initial concentration in {1, 10, 100}.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 7.
Bishop (2006), Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning, Ch 2.
